_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Child's height is 2 standard deviations (SD) or more below the mean for children of that sex and chronologic age.
Child's height is 2 SD or more below mid parental height (MPH).
Growth faltering (downward crossing of two centiles on a growth curve).
Child's height is below 3rd centile on gender and population specific growth curve.
Diagnosis of short stature is established and backed by clinical signs, symptoms, physical examination, and investigations, looking for underlying causes.

clinical_thresholds.value: -2 SD below mean for age and gender
clinical_thresholds.note: or below 2 SD of mid parental height (MPH)
on gender and population specific growth curve

preauth.label: Clinical notes – detailed history, signs & symptoms, detailed treatment line (including historical details like: since when inadequate growth observed, specific symptoms of chronic illness, antenatal history, neonatal problems, past/family/social/dietary history, medication, symptoms suggestive of early puberty)
Complete blood count, Erythrocyte sedimentation rate, Serum electrolytes, Liver function test, Kidney function test, Urine analysis, Stool analysis
Bone Age Radiograph – X-ray of left hand and wrist/Xray elbow AP is radio-graphed to assess bone maturity

(Optional) Tuberculin test and chest X-ray, thyroid hormones (T4 and TSH), blood gas analysis, tests for celiac disease (anti-endomysial and transglutaminase antibodies), Serum IGF-1, Chromosome analysis and karyotype, growth hormone provocation test, MRI brain

claim.label: Detailed ICPs with line of treatment including Establishing diagnosis/clinical improvement
Detailed Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge

ppd_preauth.q: Is the height documented less than 2 standard deviations below the mean for age?
Is there a h/o decreasing growth velocity documented in clinical notes?

common_queries: Height not documented as less than 2 standard deviations below the mean for age
Decreasing growth velocity not documented in clinical notes
Mandatory investigations (CBC, ESR, serum electrolytes, LFT, KFT, urine analysis, stool analysis) not submitted
Bone Age Radiograph (X-ray of left hand and wrist/elbow AP) missing or not properly assessing bone maturity
Clinical notes missing or incomplete regarding vital parameters, examination findings, planned line of treatment, or advice for admission

clinical_key_pointers: Short stature is a term applied to a child whose height is 2 standard deviations (SD) or more below the mean for children of that sex and chronologic age.

The most common causes of short stature beyond the first year or two of life are familial (genetic) short stature and delayed (constitutional) growth, which are normal, non-pathologic variants of growth.

The goal of the evaluation of a child with short stature is to identify the subset of children with pathologic causes (such as Turner syndrome, inflammatory bowel disease or other underlying systemic disease, or growth hormone deficiency).
The evaluation also should assess the severity of the short stature and likely growth trajectory, to facilitate decisions about intervention, if appropriate.
At any given time, a poor growth velocity is a better indicator of growth disturbance than a single point measurement.
Presenting complaint: Inability to gain height (delay of growth).

Associated symptoms: Presence of multiple systemic congenital abnormalities (dysmorphic features), Failure to thrive, Chronic illness/ Malnutrition, Recurrent respiratory infections, Diarrhea/dysentery, Joint pains and rash, Recurrent hospital admission, History of psychological abnormalities.
surgical_options: Surgical care (e.g., neurosurgical intervention for brain tumors) depends on the underlying cause of short stature.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY, often tertiary hospitals.
management_approaches: Celiac disease – gluten free diet with nutrient supplementation
Endocrinological and genetic disorders - human recombinant growth hormone

Insulin-like growth factor-I – indicated for long-term treatment of severe, primary insulin-like growth factor-I (IGF-I) due to mutations of the growth hormone receptor (GH-R) or GH-R downstream signaling pathways
Tuberculosis - Antitubercular treatment along with nutrition
Rickets - Vitamin D and C and phosphorus supplements
